sanskrit: उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः। यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः॥
transliteration: uttamaḥ puruṣas tv anyaḥ paramātmeti udāhṛtaḥ | yo loka-trayam āviśya bibharty avyaya īśvaraḥ ||
literal_meaning: But there is another, the highest Person, called the Supreme Self, the imperishable Lord who, entering the three worlds, sustains them.
contextual_meaning: Beyond the perishable body-mind and the imperishable soul-field, Krishna points to a third, trans-personal ground of awareness that silently upholds all inner and outer worlds.
psychological_state: Expansion from egoic self to witnessing awareness that contains and supports every experience without being exhausted by any.
trigger_scenario: In therapy, a client suddenly senses a calm observer behind racing thoughts; the anxiety is still there, yet it is held in a vaster, kinder space.
anchor_text: Rest as the silent awareness that holds all worlds within you.
